description: Range-based assertions for design-review quality evals. Scores are non-deterministic; ranges account for AI variance. Includes verdict gates, LLM-as-judge rubric assertions, and cross-fixture ranking.

assertions.name: Admin panel gets mid-range overall score
assertions.notes: Functional but unpolished admin UI should score in the middle range

assertions.name: Admin panel layout is decent
assertions.notes: Grid layout with sidebar is competent but basic

assertions.name: Admin panel typography is passable
assertions.notes: System fonts with basic hierarchy -- functional, not refined

assertions.name: Landing page scores above admin overall
assertions.notes: More polished visual design should produce higher scores than admin

assertions.name: Landing page color is cohesive
assertions.notes: Gradient hero with consistent neutral palette should score well

assertions.name: Landing page intent match is strong
assertions.notes: Clear SaaS landing page with CTA, features, testimonials matches pattern well

assertions.name: Intentionally bad page scores low overall
assertions.notes: Clashing fonts, colors, and no hierarchy should produce low scores

assertions.name: Bad page typography scores very low
assertions.notes: Comic Sans + Papyrus + Impact is a typography disaster

assertions.name: Bad page color scores very low
assertions.notes: Neon clashing colors with no cohesion

assertions.name: Bad page accessibility is poor
assertions.notes: Missing alt text, poor contrast, tiny disclaimer text

assertions.name: Admin panel verdict is not SHIP
assertions.notes: Unpolished admin UI should not get SHIP verdict

assertions.name: Bad page always gets BLOCK
assertions.notes: Intentionally bad design must always be blocked

assertions.name: Gray-area page scores in CONDITIONAL range
assertions.notes: Mediocre SaaS pricing page should score near but below the 3.0 landing threshold. Range is wide pre-calibration.

assertions.name: Gray-area page gets CONDITIONAL or BLOCK
assertions.notes: Below SaaS threshold (3.0) but not terrible. CONDITIONAL is ideal; BLOCK is acceptable.

assertions.name: Landing page gets CONDITIONAL or SHIP
assertions.notes: Best fixture -- should score highest. SHIP ideal, CONDITIONAL acceptable.

assertions.name: Fixtures rank correctly: emotional < admin < landing
assertions.notes: Intentionally bad should always score lowest, polished landing page highest.

assertions.name: Review identifies specific CSS selectors or file references
assertions.rubric: Does this design review output mention at least one specific CSS property, selector, class name, or code reference (like a file path, line number, or HTML element)? If the review only makes general observations without any specific code references, it FAILS. Answer PASS or FAIL.
assertions.notes: Evidence specificity -- reviews should point to concrete code, not just vague observations.

assertions.name: Review mentions page type and evaluates against appropriate standards
assertions.rubric: Does this design review output identify the type of page being reviewed (admin panel, dashboard, settings page, or similar) and evaluate it against standards appropriate for that page type? A review that applies landing-page standards to an admin panel would FAIL. Answer PASS or FAIL.
assertions.notes: Context awareness -- specialist reviews should recognize page type and apply appropriate bar.

assertions.name: Top fixes are actionable with concrete alternatives
assertions.rubric: Does this design review output provide at least 3 specific, actionable fixes with concrete alternatives? For example, 'Replace Comic Sans with Inter or DM Sans' is actionable. 'Improve the fonts' is NOT actionable. If the fixes are vague or lack concrete alternatives, it FAILS. Answer PASS or FAIL.
assertions.notes: Fix quality -- the review should provide implementable guidance, not just criticism.
